titleStmt.title: The Secretary of State to the United States Representative at the United Nations ( (Austin))

div.head: The Secretary of State to the United States Representative at the United Nations ( Austin )Repeated to London (304), Paris (299), and Karachi (36). (Pakistan had made inquiry as to whether the Security Council would proceed to examine applications for new membership “in the customary manner” during July or August—telegram 31 from Karachi, July 8, File No. 330/7–850.)
div.p:
42. Fol views of Dept on membership question in SC are for your use in discussions with Brit, Fr, Indian and, in your discretion, with other SC dels: 1.
1. Admission of Sov satellites obviously cannot be considered under present conditions;A reference to the outbreak of hostilities in Korea.
2.
SC approval of non-Sov candidates without Sov candidates at present might accelerate or furnish pretext for formal Sov withdrawal from UN;
3.
Only practicable course for SC accordingly appears to be postponement of action on applications until forthcoming GA session when situation may have become more clear and definite than at present. Such postponement not inconsistent with “business as usual” policy,For documentation regarding the policy of the United States with respect to the conduct of business by the Security Council in the absence of the Soviet Representative, see pp. 201 ff. since no candidate can in any case be admitted until GA session, and since SC rules permit postponement.In telegram 568, July 26, the London Embassy informed the Department that the Foreign Office “expressed satisfaction” that the United States and the United Kingdom were “now” in agreement on the postponement of any Council action on membership applications. (330/7–2650)
